description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who has just finished restoring a tiny
    configuration snapshot into your own home directory.  
    A compressed archive already exists at:

        /home/user/restore_test/backup_config.tar.gz

    Your job is to verify the restored files, adjust them to the correct time-zone
    and locale that the production site currently uses, and then record a validation
    log for the senior operator.

    Requirements

    1. Unpack /home/user/restore_test/backup_config.tar.gz so that the
       following two files become regular, writable files on disk (they are
       stored with the exact internal paths shown):
            restore_snapshot/etc/timezone
            restore_snapshot/etc/locale.conf

    2. Edit those two files so that they contain ONLY the lines shown below
       (no extra whitespace, no trailing spaces, no blank lines):
            restore_snapshot/etc/timezone       ➜  America/New_York
            restore_snapshot/etc/locale.conf    ➜  LANG=en_GB.UTF-8

       Notes:
       • Overwrite whatever content was restored; do NOT create new files elsewhere.
       • The files must stay inside restore_snapshot/etc/ after you finish.

    3. Produce a plain-text validation log named

            /home/user/restore_test/validation.log

       whose contents must be EXACTLY three lines in this order:

            TIMEZONE=America/New_York
            LOCALE=en_GB.UTF-8
            STATUS=OK

       – Each key, the equals sign, and the value must appear exactly as shown.
       – No extra spaces or blank lines are allowed.

    4. All of the above can be done with a very small set of terminal commands
       (roughly 3-4 commands are sufficient).  When you are done, you may `cat`
       the validation log so that the senior operator can visually confirm it.

    The automated test suite will check:

       • That /home/user/restore_test/backup_config.tar.gz remains untouched.  
       • That the two configuration files exist at the exact paths given and
         contain precisely the required single lines.
       • That /home/user/restore_test/validation.log exists and matches the
         three-line format shown above with no deviations.

    You do NOT need root privileges, and you must perform all actions inside
    your own home directory tree.

    Hint: standard shell utilities (`tar`, `echo`/`printf`, redirection, etc.)
    are sufficient.
truth:
Initial state (pre-extracted):
        • The archive /home/user/restore_test/backup_config.tar.gz exists and
          contains these files and exact initial contents:

              restore_snapshot/etc/timezone
                  └─ UTC

              restore_snapshot/etc/locale.conf
                  └─ LANG=fr_FR.UTF-8


    Desired end-of-task state for automated verification:

        1. /home/user/restore_test/backup_config.tar.gz
               – must still be present and unmodified (same checksum).

        2. /home/user/restore_test/restore_snapshot/etc/timezone
               – regular file, permissions 0644 or stricter,
                 size 16 bytes,
                 content (including newline): "America/New_York\n"

        3. /home/user/restore_test/restore_snapshot/etc/locale.conf
               – regular file, permissions 0644 or stricter,
                 size 17 bytes,
                 content (including newline): "LANG=en_GB.UTF-8\n"

        4. /home/user/restore_test/validation.log
               – regular file, permissions 0644 or stricter,
                 size 50 bytes,
                 content (including newlines):
                       "TIMEZONE=America/New_York\n"
                       "LOCALE=en_GB.UTF-8\n"
                       "STATUS=OK\n"
